plan_analysis:
The pre-research context already supplies:  
• Exact resolution rule (Yahoo Finance closing price) – no gap.  
• Historical distribution of WEC 7-trading-day returns (helpful but single dataset) – more historical context desirable.  
• Very high-level sector and macro overview – lacks detail on utilities trend in the last few weeks and degree of rate-sensitivity.  
• No information on company-specific catalysts (earnings, regulatory rulings, ex-dividend, dividend record date, guidance, storms) that could move the stock in the short 7-trading-day window.  
• No fresh news flow or analyst actions for early March 2026.  
Therefore a skilled forecaster still needs:  
1. Broader and comparative base rates: WEC and utilities sector weekly moves, daily move magnitudes, price behavior around Fed decisions and ex-dividend days.  
2. Calendar catalysts between 3 Mar and 12 Mar: scheduled corporate events, ex-dividend/record dates, regulatory filings, rating agency reviews, storm-impact alerts, macro data releases most likely to move yield-sensitive utility stocks.  
3. Up-to-the-minute company-specific and sector-level news (analyst upgrades/downgrades, fuel-price shocks, weather, political or regulatory headlines).  
4. Confirmation of any ex-dividend date before 12 Mar 2026 (a typical −$1 drop on the morning of ex-dividend could decide the binary outcome).  
5. Current utilities sector momentum, Treasury-yield trajectory, and overall market sentiment for contrarian checks.

Search strategy:  
• Use Google and Agent for longer-horizon/historical data (5 queries ≈60%).  
• Use Google News and Google for real-time catalysts and dates (3 queries ≈40%).  
• The single Agent [HISTORICAL] query will retrieve a synthesized list of upcoming catalysts plus historical price reactions.  
• Cover contrarian angle by explicitly looking for ex-dividend impact and interest-rate sensitivity.

queries_planned.intent: Obtain full historical distribution of WEC’s 7-day price changes to validate/extend base rate.
Compare utilities-sector 1-week move frequency vs WEC to set broader reference class.
Studies or regressions linking WEC price moves to changes in 10-yr yields / Fed expectations.
Historical average magnitude and timing of WEC price change around ex-dividend dates.

queries_planned.query: Identify any scheduled WEC Energy Group corporate events, earnings releases, regulatory decisions, or ex-dividend dates occurring between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-12, and summarize typical stock price moves (+/-) on such event days over the last 10 years. Also provide the base-rate frequency of >1 % daily moves for WEC during comparable 7-trading-day windows.
queries_planned.intent: One-stop synthesis of catalyst calendar and historical move sizes to quantify impact probabilities.
Surface any fresh headlines, analyst actions, weather outages, or regulatory rulings since late February 2026.
Verify if the 2026 Q1 ex-dividend falls between 3 Mar and 12 Mar, a critical short-term price driver.
Capture latest sector-wide sentiment, rate-cut expectations, and macro forces influencing utility share prices during the forecast window.
